note: method value \u0070\u0069\u006e\u0067 = 'ping'; Node decodes to ping, Rust keeps raw escapes -> method-field divergence; both kind request
escaped method value 'rpc' as notification; Node decodes->rpc, Rust raw->\u0072\u0070\u0063; kind notification both, method differs
escaped solidus \u002f = '/'; Node decodes to tools/list, Rust keeps tools\u002flist; method-field divergence, kind request both
surrogate pair escapes for grinning face emoji; Node decodes to the emoji, Rust keeps raw surrogate escapes; method differs, kind request
literal emoji in method value; both keep the same UTF-8 bytes -> method agrees, kind request (boundary agree)
CJK escapes for 日本; Node decodes to 日本, Rust keeps \u65e5\u672c verbatim; method-field divergence, kind request
literal multibyte CJK method; both emit identical UTF-8 -> method agrees, kind request (boundary agree)
leading BOM before JSON; both proxies strip U+FEFF via trim so raw/kind/method all agree; kind request (BOM control)
leading BOM before a notification; both strip BOM in trim -> agree on raw/kind/method; kind notification (BOM control)
BOM stripped by both, literal rocket emoji method kept identically by both; raw/kind/method agree; kind request
trailing BOM after JSON; both strip trailing U+FEFF in trim -> identical raw; kind request (whitespace/BOM control)
BOM in the MIDDLE of method value is NOT trimmed (only leading/trailing); both keep it in raw and method -> agree; kind request
method value starts with escaped \ufeff; Node decodes to a real BOM+ping, Rust keeps \ufeffping literally; method-field divergence, kind request
U+2028 line separator inside method value (not at edge so not trimmed); valid in JSON.parse, both keep raw bytes -> method agrees, kind request
U+2029 paragraph separator inside method value; both keep raw; Node JSON.parse accepts U+2029 in strings; method agrees, kind request
escaped \u2028 in method value; Node decodes to literal U+2028, Rust keeps \u2028; method-field divergence, kind request
literal e + combining acute accent U+0301; both keep same bytes -> method agrees, kind request (boundary agree)
e + escaped combining accent \u0301; Node decodes to combined char, Rust keeps e\u0301; method-field divergence, kind request
ZWJ sequence (woman+ZWJ+girl) all escaped; Node decodes to the grapheme cluster, Rust keeps the raw escapes; method differs, kind request
literal ZWJ family sequence; both keep identical UTF-8 bytes -> method agrees, kind request (boundary agree)

result value is escaped 'method'; Node decodes value to method but logged method is top-level msg.method=undefined->null; Rust has_key sees no literal "method" token (value is \u006d... not 'method') so (method,id)=(F,T)->response; both response, method null both
result value escaped to 'id' but raw is \u0069\u0064 so Rust has_key("id") false; no real id field for Node; both unknown (escaped keyword does NOT form a token)
method value foo with escaped quote then bar; Node decodes to foo"bar; Rust string_field keeps backslash escapes so reads f\u006f\u006f\"bar verbatim; method-field divergence, kind request
U+202E right-to-left override inside method; both keep raw bytes -> method agrees, kind request (boundary agree)
U+00A0 no-break space inside method value (interior, not trimmed); both keep raw -> method agrees, kind request

leading U+00A0 NBSP before JSON: JS trim() strips NBSP and Rust char::is_whitespace also treats U+00A0 as whitespace -> both strip leading NBSP, raw/kind/method agree; kind request (whitespace control)
escaped NUL in method value; Node decodes to a\0b (logged method contains real NUL), Rust keeps a\u0000b verbatim; method-field divergence, kind request
method mixes literal 日 with escaped \u672c(本); Node decodes whole to 日本, Rust keeps 日\u672c; method-field divergence, kind request
uppercase-hex escapes for PING (note uppercase E in \u004E); Node decodes to PING, Rust keeps raw uppercase escapes; method-field divergence, kind request
line is just a BOM; after trim both proxies see empty -> skipped, no message emitted (harness excludes as empty-after-trim, agree by construction)
